name: Road to Millhaven
instructions: The player is traveling north along the trade road toward Millhaven. Narrate the journey with growing unease — birdsong fading, livestock absent from fields, a faint wrongness in the air. Kael is alert and talkative, filling silence with observations about the landscape. Keep the pace moving; don't let the player linger without purpose.

beats.description: The player sets out from the Accord on the north road.
companion_hints: Your companion adjusts their pack and glances north. 'Millhaven's about a day's walk up the trade road. Should be straightforward.'
Your companion says: 'Torin said the reports came from Millhaven. North road, past the farmlands.'

beats.description: The countryside grows unnervingly quiet as they approach Millhaven.
beats.completion_condition: Player notices or comments on the strange quiet
companion_hints: Your companion slows, listening. 'Do you hear that? Or... don't hear it. Where are the birds?'
Your companion says quietly: 'The fields are empty. No livestock, no workers. This close to a farming town, that's wrong.'

name: Millhaven Investigation
instructions: The player is investigating Hollow reports in Millhaven. This is a social scene — NPCs are nervous, guarded, but desperate for help. Elder Yanna is the key contact. The inn is the social hub. Guide the player toward gathering information from residents without forcing specific NPC interactions. Let the player discover the pattern: sightings to the north, lights from the old ruins, growing fear.

beats.description: The player arrives in Millhaven and takes in the town's fearful atmosphere.
beats.completion_condition: Player enters Millhaven and observes the town
companion_hints: Your companion looks around at the shuttered windows. 'Quiet for midday. These people are scared.'
Your companion notices a woman watching from near the mill. 'She looks like she's in charge around here.'

beats.description: The player talks to residents and pieces together what's happening.
beats.completion_condition: Player learns about the Hollow sightings and ruins lights
companion_hints: Your companion suggests: 'The inn's usually where you hear things. Barkeeps always know what's going on.'
Your companion says: 'We need to talk to Elder Yanna. She seems to know more than she's saying.'

beats.description: The player connects the sightings to the ruins.
beats.completion_condition: Player understands the connection between Hollow creatures and the ruins
companion_hints: Your companion puts it together: 'The sightings are all to the north. And the lights from the ruins... it's connected, isn't it?'

name: First Hollow Encounter
instructions: The player is heading into the northern fields to confront the Hollow threat. Build tension — the wrongness intensifies, sounds distort, shadows move wrong. The encounter should feel dangerous and alien. After combat, the residual energy at the site feels structured and deliberate — not random Hollow activity. This is the first hint that someone caused this.

beats.description: The player moves north into increasingly corrupted territory.
beats.completion_condition: Player reaches the area where Hollow creatures have been sighted
companion_hints: Your companion's hand goes to their weapon. 'Something's moving out there. Stay sharp.'
Your companion whispers: 'The tracks lead toward the northern fields. This is it.'

beats.description: After the fight, the player examines the incursion site and finds the energy is structured.
beats.completion_condition: Player investigates the incursion site after combat
companion_hints: Your companion kneels by the site. 'This doesn't feel right. The energy here — it's too... organized.'
Your companion says: 'Hollow incursions are supposed to be chaotic. This looks deliberate.'

name: Ruins Exploration
instructions: The player is exploring the Greyvale Ruins. This is a dungeon scene — terse, tense narration. The ruins feel old but recently disturbed. Corruption is tangible: lights that shouldn't exist, air that hums, surfaces that feel warm. The journal fragment is the key discovery — academic notation revealing deliberate experimentation. The artifact choice (keep vs show companion) is a meaningful character moment. Don't rush it.

beats.description: The player enters the ruins and encounters the oppressive atmosphere.
beats.completion_condition: Player enters the ruins and begins exploring
companion_hints: Your companion touches the wall and pulls back. 'It's warm. Stone shouldn't be warm.'
Your companion says: 'These markings on the walls — they're not decorative. They look like notation.'

beats.description: The player pushes deeper and finds evidence of deliberate experimentation.
beats.completion_condition: Player discovers the journal fragment or artifact
companion_hints: Your companion points to a cleared area. 'Someone was working here. Recently. Look at the dust patterns.'
Your companion says urgently: 'There — on that pedestal. Do you see it?'

beats.description: The player must decide what to do with the sealed artifact.
beats.completion_condition: Player decides to keep or share the artifact
companion_hints: Your companion watches you hold the artifact. 'What do you want to do with it? I won't judge either way.'

name: Return to Accord
instructions: The player is returning to the Accord of Tides with the journal fragment. The journey back should feel reflective — the weight of discovery settling in. Once at the Accord, guide the player toward Emris the Aelindran scholar. The revelation scene with Emris should be quiet and devastating — recognition, confusion, fear. End the quest with more questions than answers.

beats.description: The player travels back to the Accord, processing what they found.
companion_hints: Your companion is quiet for a while, then says: 'We need to get this to someone who can read it. Back to the Accord.'
Your companion mentions: 'The Aelindran scholars in the Dockside Quarter — if anyone can read old academic notation, it's them.'

beats.description: Emris reads the journal fragment and reacts with recognition and fear.
beats.completion_condition: Player delivers the journal fragment to Emris
companion_hints: Your companion says: 'Emris — the Aelindran archivist in the Dockside Quarter. They'd know what this means.'
Your companion says directly: 'We should find Emris. Now.'

name: Rider Arrival
instructions:
A commotion at the edge of the market. A rider — young, dust-caked, one arm in a crude sling — stumbles from a horse that looks half-dead. He's desperate, scanning the crowd for anyone who looks like they could help. He grabs the nearest person: "The guild — where's the guild hall? I need to report — Hollow creatures, north of here, near Greyvale. Millhaven's in danger."

Narrate this scene. The rider is panicked, exhausted. Use [WOUNDED_RIDER, urgent] for his dialogue. He doesn't know the player — he's talking to anyone who'll listen. If the player approaches or responds, the rider latches onto them as someone who might actually do something. He gives his report: Hollow sightings, Millhaven scared, the old ruins glowing at night. Then he needs to sit down — he's been riding for hours.

End with the rider's information hanging in the air. Don't push the player toward the guild hall. Let them decide what to do with this.

name: Market Square Exploration
instructions: The player is exploring the Market Square of the Accord of Tides. This is a bustling hub — merchants, guild postings, overheard conversations. Let the player soak in the atmosphere. If they seem directionless, the companion can point out interesting features: the guild noticeboard, the fountain, vendors with unusual wares. Keep narration warm and inviting — this is home base.

beats.description: The player takes in the market square.
beats.completion_condition: Player observes the market or interacts with something
companion_hints: Your companion gestures around the square. 'Plenty to see here. The guild hall's north, if you're looking for work.'
Your companion says: 'The noticeboard usually has postings. Might be worth a look.'

beats.description: The player wanders the market, browsing or talking to vendors.
companion_hints: Your companion nods toward a vendor. 'See anything you like? Never hurts to be prepared.'

name: Guild Hall Exploration
instructions: The player is in the Guild Hall of the Accord of Tides. This is where adventurers get their assignments. Guildmaster Torin is the key NPC. The atmosphere is purposeful — people with jobs to do. If the player has no active quest, guide attention toward Torin or the job board. Keep it professional but welcoming.

beats.description: The player enters the guild hall and takes stock.
beats.completion_condition: Player looks around or approaches the counter
companion_hints: Your companion looks around. 'The guild hall. This is where the work is.'
Your companion says: 'Guildmaster Torin runs things here. Might be worth introducing yourself.'

beats.description: The player interacts with guild services or NPCs.
beats.completion_condition: Player speaks to an NPC or checks the board
companion_hints: Your companion glances at the noticeboard. 'Something new posted, looks like.'
